requirements.text: WHEN a project is read by code, id, or path, the system shall resolve one canonical project instance before returning project data.
WHEN listing projects, the system shall include projects from global registry entries and auto-discovered local configurations that are inside configured discovery paths.
WHEN a project has both registry metadata and local configuration, the system shall return a merged project view using local configuration for operational fields.
WHEN creating or registering a global-only project, the system shall persist the complete project definition in the global registry without requiring a local config file.
WHEN creating or registering a project-first project, the system shall persist discovery metadata in the global registry and operational fields in the project-local config.
WHEN creating or discovering an auto-discovery project, the system shall use the project-local config as the complete project definition without requiring a global registry entry.
WHEN updating editable project fields, the system shall write name, description, repository, and supported active-state changes to the source of truth for the project's configuration mode.
WHEN updating only a project description, the system shall persist the new description and return it on the next project read.
WHEN updating a visible project that has no registry file but has a local config path, the system shall update the local config instead of failing because the registry file is missing.
WHEN a project update succeeds, the system shall refresh public project reads so reopened UI forms and selectors show the persisted values.
WHEN a project is created or updated through one public interface, the system shall make the resulting project state observable through Web UI, API, CLI, and MCP project reads.
IF project input validation fails, THEN the system shall reject the operation with field-specific validation details and without writing partial configuration.
IF a project cannot be found by the supplied identifier, THEN the system shall return a not-found result that identifies the unresolved project key.
The system shall preserve distinct write ownership rules for global-only, project-first, and auto-discovery projects.
The system shall use a shared project contract for Web UI, API, CLI, and MCP project operations.
The system shall validate project codes, names, paths, and editable fields before mutating configuration files.
Project update tests shall verify persisted readback and not only successful command responses or UI success messages.
Project updates shall not convert a project-first minimal registry entry into a complete registry definition unless an explicit migration operation is requested.

IF registry metadata and local configuration disagree on operational project fields, THEN the system shall prefer local configuration for project-first reads and surface the inconsistency for diagnostics.
IF a file-system write fails during project update, THEN the system shall preserve the previous project configuration and report the failed write target.
